fields.summary: Represents a four-byte OpenType tag used to identify tables, axes, and other font features.
fields.remarks:
<format type="text/markdown"><![CDATA[
## Remarks

`SKFourByteTag` wraps a 32-bit value whose four bytes are interpreted as ASCII characters.
Four-byte tags are used throughout OpenType to identify font tables (e.g. `'c','m','a','p'`),
variation axes (e.g. `'w','g','h','t'` for weight), and other named features.

Create a tag from four characters using the `(char, char, char, char)` constructor, or
from a string with <xref:SkiaSharp.SKFourByteTag.Parse(System.String)>. The string
overload pads to four characters with spaces if the input is shorter.

Implicit conversions to and from `uint` are provided for interoperability.

This type is a value type and does not require disposal.

## Examples

```csharp
// Create the weight variation axis tag 'wght'
var wght = new SKFourByteTag('w', 'g', 'h', 't');

// Or parse from a string
var wghtParsed = SKFourByteTag.Parse("wght");

Console.WriteLine(wght == wghtParsed); // True
Console.WriteLine(wght.ToString());    // "wght"
```
]]></format>

fields.summary: Initializes a new instance of the <see cref="T:SkiaSharp.SKFourByteTag" /> struct from a raw 32-bit value.
Initializes a new instance of the <see cref="T:SkiaSharp.SKFourByteTag" /> struct from four ASCII characters.

fields.summary: Parses a string into an <see cref="T:SkiaSharp.SKFourByteTag" />, padding with spaces if the string is shorter than four characters.
fields.returns: An <see cref="T:SkiaSharp.SKFourByteTag" /> constructed from the first four characters of the string, padded with spaces as needed. Returns a zero tag if the string is <see langword="null" /> or empty.

params.tag: The string to parse. If <see langword="null" /> or empty, a zero tag is returned.

fields.summary: Returns the four-character string representation of this tag.
fields.returns: A four-character string where each character corresponds to one byte of the tag value.
